'''Jordan James''' (born 24 May 1980) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played for in the [[Super League]] for the [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]] along with the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]], the [[Swinton Lions]], the [[Widnes Vikings]], the [[Castleford Tigers]], the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

James is a former [[Royal Marine]] of [[42 Commando]] who served with the marines in Iraq and was awarded the King's Badge during his Marines training and played his amateur rugby league with [[Gloucestershire Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rlcm.isntweb.net/latest-news/6393-rugby-league |title=Gloucestershire Warriors running out of time |accessdate=2011-09-13 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402111146/http://rlcm.isntweb.net/latest-news/6393-rugby-league |archivedate= 2 April 2012 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/feb/01/andy-wilson-celtic-crusaders|location=London|work=The Guardian|first=Andy|last=Wilson|title=Rugby league: Jordan James ready to lead Celtic Crusaders into new territory|date=2009-02-02}}</ref>

After retiring from playing, James has gone on to have a successful career coaching and inspiring younger Wigan Warriors' players.

==Background==
James was born in [[Bath, Somerset|Bath]], [[Avon (county)|Avon]], England.

==International honours==
James has been captain numerous times for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at Salford, Sheffield, Castleford, Widnes and Celtic Crusaders.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] squad to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]] prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Crusaders dominate Wales' squad">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Crusaders dominate Wales' squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-03|access-date=2008-10-04}}</ref>

He was named [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]' player of the year for 2009.<ref name="Hayne named Player of the Year">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992|title=Hayne named Player of the Year|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=2009-11-10|accessdate=2010-04-19}}</ref>

In 2010 he represented Wales again in the [[2010 Alitalia European Cup]].

He represented Wales in the [[2011 Four Nations]].

He represented Wales in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Personal life==
He appeared in the fourth series of the [[ITV (TV network)|ITV]] dating show ''[[Take Me Out (UK game show)|Take Me Out]]'', which aired on 1 December 2012. He got a date with Victoria after becoming the first person in the history of the show to still have all 30 lights left on in the final round.{{citation needed|date=August 2016}}
